prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_svr', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.21) and high seasonality variance (0.46), with THETA dominating both overall RMSE and early horizon performance while FT_svr excels at trend tracking and late horizons. The strong upward trend across all folds combined with divergent seasonal capture patterns supports DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_svr", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "CWT_svr", "svr", "ONLY_FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.2133,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.21) and high seasonality variance (0.46), with THETA dominating both overall RMSE and early horizon performance while FT_svr excels at trend tracking and late horizons. The strong upward trend across all folds combined with divergent seasonal capture patterns supports DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage is critical (shrinkage=0.25). Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.46) and model disagreement. THETA dominates overall RMSE (8165) while FT_svr excels at trend tracking; DBA leverages complementary strengths. Relative_spread_mean=0.144 is moderate but pattern flags high disagreement, justifying robust methods like dba_combination."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No ≥4 same-type candidates. n_windows=3 means weighted candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection from past windows only. Universe leaderboards show some individual metric leaders but current set performs well overall with score_margin_top2=0.012. No redundancy detected. All candidates are valid and in current_candidate_names.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.21) and high seasonality variance (0.46), with THETA dominating both overall RMSE and early horizon performance while FT_svr excels at trend tracking and late horizons. The strong upward trend across all folds combined with divergent seasonal capture patterns supports DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage is critical (shrinkage=0.25). Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.46) and model disagreement. THETA dominates overall RMSE (8165) while FT_svr excels at trend tracking; DBA leverages complementary strengths. Relative_spread_mean=0.144 is moderate but pattern flags high disagreement, justifying robust methods like dba_combination.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No ≥4 same-type candidates. n_windows=3 means weighted candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection from past windows only. Universe leaderboards show some individual metric leaders but current set performs well overall with score_margin_top2=0.012. No redundancy detected. All candidates are valid and in current_candidate_names.
